been seeing this in my own life recently

i dont need to stop social media or decrease over-playing/over-resting to increase my productivity

i could genuinely 10x my productivity while still having shitty amounts of napping and tiktok https://t.co/J2hdi9uEPK

reminds me of the moneytwt idea that there are some people out there who r mega dumb and incompetent and who party every day and go against all the productivity/health advice, and yet theyre still magically making 100k/month or wtvr

people r Potent and Powerful

most people (at least in my social circle) only need to use like 3% of their full potential to accomplish the craziest things

as long as you focus on the right things 80/20 pareto

but everyone (me) is confusd on those things

how i want to live

for a long time i was focused on making myself productive (and failing btw) so i could make like 10k/month or wtvr

recently i said idc anymore fuck it we ball

just tried “living more gloriously”

“do whatever you want” is 0iq/9999iq https://t.co/ugn2Kv17W9

right now im making $0 but i have more zest for life — not only do i enjoy life more but i also feel more potent and capable of casting things i want into existence

if you find a Why for life, the How will naturally take care of itself

but everyone is so caught up in the (unimportant) motions of the How, and usually they don’t even serve an invigorating authentic Why

so they are doing ineffective inefficient actions that limp forward towards life paths that arent even fulfilling

be more silly
be more chaotic

allow serendipity to cultivate

that can only happen when youre not so rigid about how your life “should” be

success actually has a Higher Chance of occurring when you just say “fuck it we ball” and you just do whatever the fuck you want

but the catch is

it prob wont be the success you originally wanted

but the saving grace is

it’s prob even better than what you originally wanted

bringing it back to my original example in my own life

im making $0 rn but i know im increasing my magical power to make money

its not discipline

but the fact that i have more vigor for my desires

in woowoo terms, i have more magical zest and higher energy to attract success

and more importantly, money isnt a fixation anymore

im making $0 rn but im not shaming myself for being unproductive

cant force things against the flow of the river

willpower and discipline is mid iq
fuck it we ball is 0iq/9999iq

i am trying to live a more 0iq/999iq life

a text i sent my bf —

“this isnt just like oh youll feel better if you do the left/right side and not the middle iq

but it’s also like https://t.co/BbmNDkjJVY

youre set up for failure if youre doing the mid iq one AND its acc almost impossible to even do the mid iq one like as a law of human nature / a law of the universe it just doesnt work that way thats just not how shit gets done”

gratitude journaling doesnt only make you more frequently/habitually positive about individual moments

but by keeping a coherent log of your daily life (and positively reflecting on it), you start positively viewing your life in a Holistic sense.

ive started to keep a log of things that im grateful for, and now im not only grateful for XYZ moments but im also grateful for the past few days as a Whole or the past two weeks as a Whole

my life as a whole feels more meaningful and more integrated (in the erik erikson sense)

i mean integration as in the opposite feeling of regret

some sort of appreciation or peace or gratitude or satisfaction or correctness or homeness

integration in the sense of how erik erikson used the word when describing his last stage of psychosocial development

realizing that happiness can be actively cultivated in small but potent ways

i dont need to go to flight school or travel the world to be fulfilled

apparently going to the driving range or hanging out w friends is just as amazing

i just didnt allow myself to do those before
bc i was so fixated on happiness being stored away in some far off goal

and yes it has led to some more unhealthier actions like more tiktok more overeating etc

doing wtvr i want has led me to be more social and try new activities

@the_wilderless peterson has this interesting model of what art (among other things) comprises

its the Dreamed/Imagistic realm that treads the line between the articulated (logical, known, etc) and the rest of the procedural (physical, everything else we dont know, etc) https://t.co/xzp5rq3EVH

this but replace “survival tactics” with Discipline and replace “connection” with Passion

to be open to your true authenticity, you have to minimize all repression and rigidity which means being open to unhealthy desires https://t.co/kdUNfBeMBQ

to truly explore the extremely good parts of your authenticity (eg passions) you have to be somewhat open to the bad parts too (eg drugs, tantrums, etc)

accept the shadow and you will receive light
if youre not open to both sides, you will hold on to some rigidity — which will keep you safe from the dark but it will also limit you from getting the coolest forms of light

Do Whatever You Want.

steve jobs and drug addicts are (almost) the same type of person — they just do whatever they want every day and they are invigorated by it

unhealthy impulses r very similar to healthy invigorations

but it’s not an answer you have to find right now, nor is it even an Experiment to get closer to the perfect answer — it’s just Life

and that sort of “experimentation” frame also limits the experiments to whatever question youre confining yourself to ask — what if after this experiment, you actually wanna ask a different question?

perhaps the coolest and most soulful thing to do is to just let go of the entire question in the first place .. and to just Live Right Now

the answer will never be perfect — and neither will the question. your task is to follow your excitement anyway

your task is to accept the call

“oh no what if i choose the wrong path?” — it is inevitable. you WILL choose the wrong path. but only if you were looking for a “right path” in the first place.

just dance with whatever resonates with you Right Now

when you sing a song, you do not stop and ponder “oh no what if i dont wanna sing this specific genre for the rest of my life???” NO!! YOU JUST SING OUT!!!

when you indulge in some playlist at 4pm in the afternoon, you do not stop and ponder “oh no what if i wanna listen to a different genre tomorrow…” NO!! YOU JUST START LISTENING TO THE SONG BECAUSE THE ART GRABS YOU AND PULLS YOU IN
when you start bopping to a song, you do not stop yourself and ponder “oh no what if i dont wanna bop to jazz for the next month??” NO!! YOU JUST DANCE BECAUSE THE MUSIC COMPELS YOU!!!!!

so WHY do you stop and question yourself when dancing to the music of your SOUL??

just allow yourself to act on whatever feels the most compelling

this isnt a thinking act, it’s more of a feeling act - what does your body feel pulled to do?

emphasis on the PULL

the question is NOT “what do i feel like doing right now?”

the question is actually “what do i feel PULLED to do right now??”

it’s actually less of You Doing, and more of you Allowing energy to flow through you and animate your existence

when you dance to a song, you rarely use a conscious will to probe the inquiry “oh how does my body feel like moving?”

you actually just Allow the body to move in the way it naturally wants to

its like you submit to some other force that flows through your body

so when you dance to the music of your soul, there are no right answers, and no questions in the first place

it’s not a function of Will, but a function of Allowing yourself to be consumed by the ebbs and flows of the music of the Universe
it’s not a question of what You should Do, but how can you Allow divine energy to flow through you and animate your existence so that you surrender to the Playful Dance

im able to do this for things like Math -- im able to ace all my math tests not bc i can memorize an arsenal of equations (im actually shit at memorization) but bc i actually understand the underlying logic behind the Math Itself

for math i'm able to understand the underlying One Reality of How Things Are -- and all the solutions become obvious and straightforward, so much so that usually whenever i see a wrong solution i'm able to immediately suss it out and my body even reacts with a disgust response
i'm not like extremely mega ultra genius at math but i'd say i'm at least top 0.1%

and all the ppl i know who are rly good at math do it the same way - they don't memorize tactics or strategies or equations,, they just Understand Math and they know how to do that, and everything becomes Immediately Obvious or Straightforward from there
and something tells me this is exactly how steve jobs approached business

people who don't Get It will try to turn steve jobs' approach to business into a set of strategies or tactics to emulate his success

but what they don't understand is that it isn't just a tactic that you can compartmentalize - he had a completely different way of seeing things

hehe now i get to write a thread on how this happened for me

it was a lot of things but i wanna try listing the main ones https://t.co/rEB87og2ab
first of all wanna say idk what will work for other ppl depending on their situation but like . this is just what seemed to rly help me out

- realized that being hyperrational was fucking me over and i was so stuck in my Left Hemisphere (shoutout river kenna), realizing a lot of it was based on shame + fear, took baby steps to dissolve it by treating it like OCD or an addiction to certainty

- every time something threatened my epistemological base, i felt Doomed and Fucked . then realized i can never have a satisfactory epistemological base, so i allowed myself to not need to be certain/epistemological/rational about every single thing, even if it was uncomfortable

silly meme but very related:
https://t.co/DvVPL9e6KM
- also realized my epistemological base was never even that good in the first place bc obviously i was living with such a doomed wretched emotional experience

- literally just became more normal ... hung out more w my friends, stopped thinking that i had some esoteric superior knowledge, trusted their normal thoughts even if it wasnt "logical"
- took a break from TPOT, philosophy, etc. stopped being so rational/philosophical in general
- stopped trying to apply mathy logic to non-mathy things

- i rly loved yatharth's tweets abt cptsd and river kenna's tweets about LH/RH, and i tried to take that knowledge and actually integrate it into my life
https://t.co/XiuUzo3zGh

i need to emphasize the hanging out with friends part ... that stuff was one of the most impactful things i've done in my lil "healing" journey so far .. just being normal and having fun and connecting with people and not trying to intellectualize everything and just bantering

- realized i was sort of "addicted" (in a sense) to the extreme doom/stress that i would feel, or the euphoria-dysphoria cycle in general. the book "existential kink" touches on this subject in a fascinating way, but can also be explained by normal psych

- realized that euphoria n dysphoria are two sides of the same coin (me being neurotic + me looking at things in black n white) so the goal isnt to achieve that perfection then become euphoric from not being broken anymore, but instead realize u were never broken to begin with

- metta meditation + in general trying to integrate that pov onto the world/myself, trying to extend unconditional love n compassion to myself, trying to be nicer to myself, remember i am Fundamentally Okay and Good

a lot of this process was a back n forth dialectic betw hyperrational -> go with the flow, follow impulses -> oh wait actually it's not black n white, we can balance the two -> adding more LH/RH depending on what i need at this stage

realized that lots of progress can be made with baby steps

used to think i was absolutely broken if i had some flaw, and also thought i could never change it -- usually it was just an overreaction and just took 5-10 days of extremely small baby steps to snowball into smth lovely

> if the uphill path is exponential then what that means is it doesnt matter how big the first steps you take uphill are, even if theyre trivial, even if theyre shameful in their size, if ur disciplined in that, youll speed up extraordinarily rapidly
https://t.co/i5k8Q1AuXg
tried catching myself whenever i thought in Extremes, then reminded myself i probably just have some unmet emotional need right now or smth which is making me think like that

i agree but i'd give a small edit to this - baby steps

yes do more than you think you can do, but nothing crazy .. consistently take seemingly trivial baby steps which are always just outside of your comfort zone .. it will snowball slowly but surely https://t.co/gX4RyLqvyj

otherwise tbh if i did anything larger than a baby step, i would just sort of burn myself out and not do anything for the next week or month and then i'd be back to square one and it would be a loop of no progress, and i was stuck in that loop for a while

just like in the gym, you cant just always try to hit a PR - obv it's not sustainable and it'll even hurt you! and it's just not how progress works

same w mental game imo

progressive overload works by pushing yourself just outside of what is currently possible

sometimes that looks like just doing 1 pushup or doing 15 secs of meditation or studying for 5 mins -  then within a week that would somehow compound to like 5-50x the initial "trivial" amt

that was the only way progress ever happened for me personally, through seemingly trivial baby steps

took me a while to learn that i cant just always compare myself to my best - just like you cant always try to hit near-PR levels in the gym (esp if u havent gym'ed for a while!)

im doing a writeup on this topic rn but lemme just list some common pitfalls off the top of my head

1. sorta trying to teleport to the end outcome of peace/joy/equanimity/etc by trying to change your mind like “i just gotta go w the flow!” “i should just accept this!” etc, which is kinda super ineffective

i just recently found out some people label this as “end-gaining”

@techno0ptimist @BecomingCritter another way it shows up in pedagogy:

https://t.co/ON3cTrsrAA

2. trying to remove bad emotions

it turns out suffering is produced by our resistance to things, not the actual bad emotion, and so the goal isnt to remove bad emotions (youre still human) but to lower resistance/clinging/attachment

and not in a philosophical way but like literally physiologically there are particular 10-40hz graspy clingy mental motions which turn out to be the cause of ~all suffering, which we can learn to investigate and undo

(and this is what “desire is the root of all suffering” really means, but it’s widely misinterpreted bc of bad translation — it’s not a philosophical argument, it’s super verifiable via meditation bc of things like sensory clarity)

@techno0ptimist @BecomingCritter 2.5 trying to remove bad emotions via trying to fight/delete/repress/shoo them

it turns out this is just super ineffective, and the way to dissolve these things is more about letting them pass without constipating it (again, it’s about less resistance) and bringing them to the…

2.5 trying to remove bad emotions via trying to fight/delete/repress/shoo them

it turns out this is just super ineffective, and the way to dissolve these things is more about letting them pass without constipating it (again, it’s about less resistance) and bringing them to the light and putting awareness on it (which sometimes can look like “love”)

ok im sorta simplifying but yeah basically

@techno0ptimist @BecomingCritter 3. directly doing insight meditation without untangling psychological gunk first

it turns out this is one of the common ways to do meditation unsafely (bc people get rly ungrounded n disoriented after experiencing things like oh my sense of self is an illusion, etc), but one of…

3. directly doing insight meditation without untangling psychological gunk first

it turns out this is one of the common ways to do meditation unsafely (bc people get rly ungrounded n disoriented after experiencing things like oh my sense of self is an illusion, etc), but one of the ways it seems to be remedied by first laying a foundation by cleaning up your mind via good therapy (IFS, coherence therapy, gendlin focusing, etc) and enjoyable meditation like metta, jhanas

3.5 grinding “just focus on the breath”

yeah it turns out this is like the meditation equivalent of going to a tennis coach every day but not fundamentally improving bc you’re just hitting the ball and not really debugging the fundamental micromovements that matter

the techniques are just scaffolding to allow the tower to fall properly because otherwise we as humans are so neurotic n confused that pedagogical prescriptions like “just let go” (etc) often actually does not lead to nondoing but often actually just creates more tension/doing bc we try to artificially manufacture the letting go and empirically it seems almost impossible to Just Let Go without actual precise techniques

i think a good analogy could be smth IFS-textured, like imagine your inner world is a bunch of silly clumsy neurotic monkeys tugging/pulling/pushing at each other, and sure ideally you could just tell them to all let go and they’d let go, but in practice that kind of pedagogy/metalearning is super empirically ineffective, bc telling the neurotic clumsy monkeys to “just relax” actually gets parsed as “oh so lets delegate one monkey to force/tug/fight/suppress the other monkeys into relaxing” which obviously in the analogy leads to more tension/doing — so we need super precise instructions as scaffolding to help the neurotic clumsy silly monkeys to actually relax

this is why it can take months to years of work (even with rly good SOTA pedagogy)

to put it in chris' terms of "local optima", the premise of buddhism is that everyone is at local optima in the early layers of their psych neural network, and it turns out that fixing this specific local optima is kinda the only known solution for curing suffering and improving baseline wellbeing many orders of magnitude more than we thought possible

https://t.co/wwTwPuTSbu

> [from @nowtheo] What are some flaws or limitations of IFS?
> 
> imo it’s like the mid-layer in the psychology neural network, at a fairly complex level of abstraction. But there’s also a lot of value in the earliest layers, carefully attending to “raw” perception itself. I think that’s the path to curing yourself of suffering. Higher upside
> 
> But if you want to do things like change your identity in specific ways it doesn’t make sense to look at direct raw perception. It’s too fine grained. You want something higher up, which IFS is great for

https://t.co/wNexRwLlN3

> theory: therapy has marginal return to burning karma (phenomenological contractions) bc you’re doing things manually one at a time focused on stories and content, and at some point you’ve burned through the easy wins
> 
> meditation has exponential return to burning karma, bc over time you’re using awareness and equanimity (universal solvent for contractions) to dissolve it. When the arrow of attention goes away as you become more centerless this process is parallelized across all areas of your body and across all of time as the difference between meditation and life dissolves. And as the contractions get dissolved, awareness in some sense gets stronger and more decentralized, which makes this process happen faster, hence exponential

---

and so what does this local optima look like?

the buddhist claim is that there exist these particular 10-40hz particular precise mental motions ("tanha") which are the cause of ~all suffering ("dukkha").

("tanha" gets translated as desire which is a terribly antihelpful translation, but some other better approx translations are like clenching/grasping, but seems important to remember that it's not merely like macro-scale emotions/motions, it's a very precise micro event that happens several/many times per second)

and the reason we're slapping ourselves 10-40 times a second is bc it's perpetuated by some phenomenological illusions / lack of sensory clarity. ("avijja")

---

and this is not a philosophical or subjective argument, it's really an empirically reproducible phenomenological report.

https://t.co/Shr6wVqnXX

> I can’t emphasize enough how empirical this is, they’re not bc of anyone’s ideas of how things should work, it just seems to work this way, it’s been replicated throughout all of human history across different cultures, way more than most (any?) of our psych science studies

https://t.co/53m6F9IIw5

> the meditation let go thing isn’t a philosophical argument, it’s not saying you should let go for some logical reason, it’s saying that letting go empirically leads to some great things and it’s worth checking it out for yourself

---

> judgement imo breaks into a subsecond flinch (tanha) and a longer term (maybe few seconds) judgment, intellectual or felt, like a system 1 & 2 of emotion, the sub second flinch goes away, along with the suffering, system 2 stays and gets clearer bc flinch was hogging attention

https://t.co/jhVItxpZfz

> My preferred translations of tanha and dukkha are fast-grabby-thing and evil-vibrating-blob
> 
> Makes it obvious what kind of thing to look for to see them. Translations like clinging & suffering make them sound like abstract ideas. They’re not, they’re fast subtle mental movements
> 
> There’s not much to say about the evil vibrating blob other than that it’s evil — it’s the reason we think things are bad — and it’s vibrating asymmetrically, bc asymmetry lowers valence. It’s the mind’s evolved way of injecting suffering. It doesn’t really have other properties
>

>

and how do jhanas fit into the picture?

so the real point of meditation is that its one empirically reproducible path to get out of that local optima in the early layers of the psych neural network (stop "dukkha" which is caused by "tanha" which is caused by illusions / low sensory clarity). and it's not just one way to extinguish suffering, but basically it's kinda the *only* way we currently know how to undo tanha (as of now, before AGI or someone like michael edward johnson gets to invent some cool tech)

and so it turns out that jhanas are not only a powerup on the path, but also are a staircase to "cessation" (blinking out for a bit, usually only like a couple seconds), and cessation is incredibly helpful for the path bc it allows us to sorta see the reboot of the psych operating system, it's one of the things that allows for good sensory clarity to investigate tanha

(and even kiddie jhanas get us there, according to nick 2025)

https://t.co/0skD56lhGi

> I mostly don’t care about the wars over what counts as a real jhana vs kiddie jhana bc jhanas are so much less important than cessation. the jhanas are a staircase to cessation, which then teaches you things about yourself. and even kiddie jhanas can take you all the way
>
> the jhanas are a distraction from the core insight all of meditation is trying to point you to, they’re just the most useful bc they tend to be a good pointer. after you get the pointer you can go back and do mega firework jhanas for fun if you’d like

https://t.co/IPBC5lHfWv

> oh forgot to mention cessation matters imo bc the moment post cessation is about as good as you’ll ever find for studying consciousness, the reboot from 0 in slow motion the way it often is is a great place to be for finding insights. And the reboot itself often installs upgrades

https://t.co/bpJoQpCbim

> the eventual goal is insight, which has to be done via investigating direct experience. It’s the opposite of wireheading (less learning, agency) it’s a scientific tool

like . Okay i think my bottleneck was generating/sparking a positive emotion

- i've tried forgiveness stuff, worked sometimes in the past / doesn't feel alive rn
- same with metta
- same with EK but worked less

it turns out the upstream bottleneck was going from bleh/ick/UGH to neutral (like i would just sit to meditate but i'd feel like shit and i'd wanna get up)

and recently i improved a lot at easing my (resistance to) negative emotion

i found out i can sorta "breathe in" negative tension (cf. IFS "unblending"? gendlin "making space"?) and i can "give it to the ball" and then the suffering sometimes significantly goes down (and can even get to the point where it just feels like mildly uncomfy sensations)
i feel like i have weird analogies for things but that's how i like to describe it to myself https://t.co/u3uEIyYo84

and then ok that significantly helped w the negative-to-neutral bottleneck

but i was still hovering around slightly-negative or neutral, and couldnt figure out how to loop from there

then i was like Oh hm it's almost like i'm trying to find a magical button to create positive emotion, i'm doing the "end-gaining" thing rn, oops

i just need to build mastery exactly like how i think abt pedagogy for math, piano, language learning, tennis, etc

then i was like Hm Ok what's an actual deterministic way to create positive emotion instead of me just trying to press an imaginary magical button, what's an actual mental motion i can do to reproducibly create positive emotion

and, as it turns out, having tsundere-jhana-guide-asmr in your ear is actually kinda effective at generating positive emotion LMAO

maybe kinda hacky but it works nontrivially

https://t.co/vW3hpFeQaV
and then from there i was able to sort of "give it to the ball" and "be friends with it" and "chew on it" and "grapple with it"

idk if these analogies are useful/helpful except personally, but to me they refer to specific mental motions that are familiar to me when i look at the mental micro-movements/mechanisms that happen when i'm learning math/chinese/piano/tennis

anyway, after "giving [positive emotion] to the ball" i'm sort of able to go deeper into it like i would during a recreational silly auto-hypnosis session

and it feels rly familiar to the mental movements of when i was trying to learn overtone singing

going deeper into the positive emotion, sort of swimming through it until it resonates

just like going deeper into the sound and exploring it and moving your mouth shape until it resonates and the overtones can be heard

and then it also feels like Whoa owen was right about savoring negative emotion

like . whoa i can just go into it and it's just so .. Savorable???? it feels like first glance it makes me flinch but then i excitedly-curiously look at it and wipe away the dust/dirt and it's joy

like WTF??? i'll see a worrying tension bubble up, then i'll notice my flinch to it, then i'll excitedly-curiously "breathe it in" and "chew on it" and "wipe away its dust" and then there's just like EXCITEMENT UNDERNEATH?

what???????????

with jhourney we saw that it takes <100 hours of practice to learn the skill of on-demand profound joy/bliss/peace (jhanas 1-4)

with “fetters inquiry” we’re seeing that it could also take <100 hours to unlearn emotional suffering forever (“breaking reactivity”) https://t.co/Nx4HIEdtFn

a frame i’ve been trying on recently is smth like

im speculating that jhanas 1-4 and breaking reactivity might be as easy as if it just came from some random redditor on r/LucidDreaming explaining how he achieved reliable lucid dreaming after a month of 1hr/night practice

“it just came from some random redditor, he probably described it in a weird personal way, i should just personally experiment”

that frees up so much rigid expectation of what the things “should” look like, allows for more openness/curiosity/play/tinkering to discover it myself https://t.co/4uxz61sRiU
the analogy also makes clear that it’s more akin to debugging a physical technique, like learning a tennis forehand or learning how to wiggle your ear https://t.co/TZ0pVlubki
and the analogy also frees up so much seriousness about how much time n effort it should take https://t.co/kDRpbmlbMe

idk if the frame is useful for others but i’m drawn towards just viewing it like

“very decoupled from buddhism, just 0.01% of what eNLigHtEnMeNt actually is, this is rly just a terrible oversimplification of buddhism, it's just a cool silly easy hack that the body/brain can do”

as easy as overtone singing or lucid dreaming or drawing or running 10 miles

but even this cool silly lil hack (jhourney jhanas + fetters inquiry) somehow has rly profound effects just bc everyone else in the world is just terribly wrong abt the physics of happiness

it’s like

rn these are usually seen as some advanced thing that historically took hundreds or thousands of hours

and so right now “beginner meditator” means youve done like 0-500hrs of breath meditation and maybe you’ve made some slightlyyy noticeable improvement in wellbeing

but for future meditators, it could totally be the case where “beginner meditator” means you dont have reliable profound bliss + you havent gotten rid of approximately all of your conventional suffering, and it’s expected that you take only a few months to do so
it could totally be the case that jhanas 1-4 + breaking reactivity are just “noobie gainz” in the future https://t.co/9WsZjHp3sF

it feels like so much of my rumination / longlasting sadnessanxiety blobs / negative spirals required the fact that my inner thoughts were so big in experience and so sticky/viscous and so attached to the little guy up in my head. but now the inner thoughts are so faded in the background, and way less sticky/viscous, and way less of some Me with sticky hands grabbing onto the anxiety and thoughts and fueling the rumination. it's like i'm constantly in a flow state of performing music -- like there's still anxiety and negative thoughts etc, but you cant exactly ruminate on them very well bc theyre just faded in the background and most of your attention is on playing/singing the song so you just dont have the bandwidth to grab onto the negative thoughts and make them sticky and ruminate. there's still anxiety but it's very faded in the background and it's hard to get into a negative spiral bc you're mostly absorbed in the music and just flowing. it's like that but there is no song!

there’s so many examples of outlier good pedagogy where the best technique is literally just Seeing Clearly

math, meditation, drawing, therapy…

you just See Extremely Clearly, Look Really Closely, and the solution straightforwardly energes as extremely obvious

you dont argue w it, you just become so intimately acquainted w it that its wrongness just clearly obviously pops out

and the right answer just emerges as “oh of course”

you dont wanna ask loaded questions where youre already suggesting the solution, you wanna ask the questions that facilitate intimate acquaintance bc thats what makes the solution just naturally pop out with such obviousness

asking the loaded question is the same as trying to just believe the answer, it's a shortcut that doesnt work, you're basically implicitly just saying "[answer], right?" -- you cant do that, you have to ask the questions that Facilitate intimate acquaintance

it's sorta kinda like a good pset (that makes you think in ways that you get intimately acquainted w the material) vs a bad pset (where you can just go through the motions)

(( although ideally you wanna be using even bad psets as good psets, but i digress ))
i think it also rly helps to be open to having the old understanding broken / being humble / etc. this is hard if youre neurotic and clinging to the old understanding

that can happen in math with some ppl who r insecure abt their understanding etc idk wtvr

but it seems to be super common in self-inquiry bc those beliefs are Kinda Literally neurotic clinging (and/or at least mainly perpetuated by it)

we probably have 100+ publicly available world-class explainers for math/physics/cs (there are so many public resources to rec to a confused student) but we have almost 0 for the surprising empirical claims of advanced meditators

this is important bc it seems like we have the frontier empirically reproducible protocol towards profound peace/joy, and the frontier tractable micro-mechanistic understanding of what generates suffering/happiness

but how are we gonna get people to research this if no one understands it? how are we gonna get smart nerds to dedicate their lives to this if it just gets shoved away as some illegible woo weird thing? we need to be so careful and skillful at sharing this, but almost no one is doing a good job of this.

we can do so much better, there is so much low-hanging fruit to harvest here.

i think there's a nontrivial chance that none of the current frontier advanced meditation researchers will make any of the Future Big Breakthroughs towards mass-scale profound wellbeing (or, even if we do, we'll probably need to recruit lots of help along the way) -- we are so early, and everything in the field is so protoscientific right now. so, in some sense, a lot of research work in this field is just laying some good groundwork for the *future's* meditation researchers

but the problem is that those future researchers won't exist unless we do a really good job of sharing this stuff to them

fundamental research is incredibly important to do right now, but it's also really important to do a really skillful job of talking about this stuff!!!

especially in a world where thinking is getting increasingly commoditized (with ai models getting more capable of novel science), perhaps the most important leverage point for this field is making it legible/legitimate to curious people in the first place

that's what i'm trying to do with my blog post series, albeit it's just a first attempt.

we have a real opportunity here to accelerate the field by getting really good at making it legible and legitimate to curious nerds.

i'd make a different argument if the meditation research field was bigger, or if this field already had more legitimate connotations, or if nerds already thought that this field is cool -- but none of these things are true at a big scale yet.

there is so much low-hanging fruit here, and this won't happen unless we actively try to get better at it!

text:
the meditation field needs more people posting about how Fun and Beautiful it is to practice

not just about the beauty of absorbing attention into incredible amounts joy/peace, and not just about how it can radically change our everyday experience and make us feel super peaceful..

there are lots of beautiful results, but we can also talk about how the process can be so beautiful too!

this is common in music and sports and friendship. theres so much talk about not just the end product but also the beauty of late nights befriending your instrument, playing with sounds, struggling with chords. there’s so much talk about not just winning a tennis game but also the beauty of befriending the ball and the pleasure of swinging a racket. there’s so much talk about not just finally becoming best friends but also the beauty of doing the fun dance of small talk, getting to know someone new.

we can do the same for meditation!

i think one of the reasons people are so graspy about results is bc no one talks about how fun it can be to practice!!!!!!! it can be fun!! it can be SO fun!!!!

we can normalize this

it feels like meditation is in a phase right now where the common sentiment is to treat it like classical music — read some sheet music (instructions) and get good at playing the song like that to produce a beautiful result. do deliberate practice on your scales like a chore. have good posture.

orrrr you could be a jazz musician! you can be friends with music, listen really closely and intimately to it, and start babbling. crawl, feel, stumble. make your own sentences! doodle around! explore! befriend the playground. become comfortable! develop real conversational fluency!

i think we can make a similar shift of perspective in meditation practice, for jhana practices and insight practices and other practices!

text:
okay so 9 months ago i realized that people actually know quite a lot about the micromechanics of unconditional peace [1] and i also found @johnsonmxe's "vasocomputation" hypothesis [2]

[1] https://t.co/LdA1nu1l6X
[2] https://t.co/7ZWxm00qb7

but i was a wee little boy who didn't know much about anything (i think i still am) but i think i'm finally starting to kindaaa sortaaa understand what's going on!

so here's my current understanding of some things:

vasocomputation is a theory largely rooted in the perspective of sensorimotor development / embodied cognition, and i imagine that it would have to agree with a lot of other domain-invariant principles of sensorimotor metalearning

21:30 - 21:55 "When we're born, we're born into incredible sensory chaos (that William James calls this blooming buzzing confusion) and things are just happening, it's like a huge dose of LSD all the time, and it's hard to navigate. And one of the crucial tools we have available to us is clenching our smooth muscles to stabilize patterns."

this seems true of all domains of sensorimotor skill development that require learning effective motor patterns from scratch!

to loosely use a gradient descent analogy: our behavior vector starts at some random initialization, and as a beginner we can only make gross calibrations [3], but we can increasingly refine and calibrate towards skillful mastery

the example coming to mind right now is: in second language acquisition, you start out with saying X to grossly mean a first-order approximation of something, then through experience/feedback you make many increasingly refined calibrations, developing the fluency to say X to mean something more hi-fi/accurate/nuanced

[3] this seems at least partially significantly because we can only perceive low-fidelity gradients, i.e. our perceptual space starts out as a bad low-dimensional projection of reality, and also needs refinement/development; it is also a motor skill, see https://t.co/8Dz9GtJoAs (as an example of refining perception, think about increasingly nuanced interoception in tennis training, or increasingly nuanced color differentiation in painters, or increasingly nuanced felt-senses of word meanings when learning a new language)

anyway, it seems like tanha/upadana are particularly interesting instances of these gross calibrations, in the sensorimotor game of life

my mental model is something like:
physical pain, emotions, preferences are kinda like the rewards on a gridworld RL environment (in general these can all be healthy and human, and can exist post-tanha/post-upadana) -> tanha(thirst) as a certain unskillful implementation of preferences, specifically the kind that leads to upadana -> upadana(grasping) as a certain unskillful implementation of agency/control towards those preferences, characterized by grabbygrab/clinging/etc

the interesting thing is that tanha/upadana feels *incredibly bad* -- even though it might cause 99%+ of the suffering, it is just <1% of the motor pattern of playing the game of life (we know from advanced meditators that skillfully navigating life and holding wholesome relationships etc still has to be trained and cultivated even after incredible meditative development). the analogy that comes to mind is something like: imagine you're a tennis player who grips too strongly in an odd way such that you get a terrible blister every time you played tennis, and if you fix your harmful grip you'll feel a lot better, and it may make someee things a lot easier, but also obviously this would not be a miracle change to the vast majority of your technique or scores/rating or etc

but, why did this very-sucky-feeling habit get implemented?

first, a small detour to an analogy:
- a child's behavior + world model have an initialization that is very bad/inaccurate/unskillful/etc
- we can look at the sensorimotor domain/game of how parents have the responsibility of developing/cultivating/calibrating a child's behavior and world model
- in this process, the parental algorithm commonly (maybe necessarily? not sure) takes an implementation where they take advantage of the child’s ~death-aversion to produce intensely negative feedback to brutely enact gross calibrations in behavior + world model (i.e. i am talking about some forms of parental discipline)
- but then you can generally stop using these brute gross calibrations when the child’s behavior patterns are more skillful, mature, safer, etc + when the child has a good sense of danger proxies and so you can update their model via language/etc instead of physical simulation of danger (shouting, hitting, etc)
- obviously, even in a very skillful parenting environment, the child will grow up to have inaccurate priors about the world and will not act perfectly skillfully. but in less skillful parenting environments, there will be more inaccurate priors, and these inaccuracies might often be especially related to things feeling more deathly dangerous than they actually are (as a byproduct of the aforementioned calibration algorithm implementation)
- this specific algorithm/implementation of parenting, especially in higher intensities with lots of shaming or physical violence etc, is perhaps not ideal but is **incredibly easy-to-find (search and setup for better algorithms is very nontrivial and costly)**

i imagine that tanha/upadana is somewhattt like a micro version of this kind of thing! a gross brute implementation of developmental calibrations (unskillful in some sense but it was the easiest to find, and was necessary during early development; as @KanizsaBoundary says: "correct solutions to cognition take nontrivial knowledge and setup, which is also not free")

and then @johnsonmxe's vasocomputation argument is that the computation and world model etc are using vascular smooth muscle cells ("taṇhā as a particular default bias in the computational-biochemical tuning of the human nervous system, and upādāna as the impulsive physical (VSMC) clenching this leads to")

but tanha/upadana are different from the parenting analogy in a few very significant + very interesting ways:

1) calibrations to a child's personality/behavior/model seem to live in something in a more continuous space or a more densely connected graph like some n-dimensional tower of hanoi (borrowing from @meditationstuff analogy); but tanha/upadana seems to have a largely discrete ontology as we see advanced meditators describe some persistent phenomenological shifts that lean relatively more discrete (e.g. @RogerThisdell's stage model) (sure, it's not fully discrete, but this seems way more discrete than making calibrations in personality/behavior...? maybe these two things actually have similar ontologies but just undergo phase shifts at different scales? etc idk yet)

2) in the parenting / child development scenario, there's a sense in which you can think of the child as receiving a "hand-me-down" world model from the parent, kinda like the parent is hardcoding gridworld values; but for tanha/upadana, there is no "hand-me-down" of specific gridworld values, so the problem seems kinda categorically different? tanha/upadana seem to lean towards being a global parameter / globally applied pattern. yes it seems the case that you can selectively reduce tanha/upadana for certain situations, but we also definitely see global shifts in experience where practitioners undergo mostly-global reductions in tanha/upadana. Hmmmm.....

3) why is tanha/upadana deeply connected with the sense of self, in many different ways? the sense of a central epistemic agent seems to be incredibly instrumental in perpetuating tanha/upadana. the recent preprint from Shawn Prest et al is really interesting (https://t.co/oS7S4AsBGo) and i'm glad there are papers nowadays trying to computationally formalize the difference between the identified-with self (the *sense* of a central epistemic agent, etc; which seems to be a generated overlay onto experience, and removal does not *necessarily* lead to organism dysfunction) versus the organismic self (e.g. that which Levin talks about here: https://t.co/xx6gzH5jOM)

and of course there's many other questions... but will sign off for now!

(i need more phenomenological sensory clarity, mathematical machinery, and/or biophysics knowledge to figure out more... and/or i wanna talk to more people about this... and i also wanna start prioritizing my own meditative practice/development a bit more)

raw.full_text:
okay so 9 months ago i realized that people actually know quite a lot about the micromechanics of unconditional peace [1] and i also found @johnsonmxe's "vasocomputation" hypothesis [2]

[1] https://t.co/LdA1nu1l6X
[2] https://t.co/7ZWxm00qb7

but i was a wee little boy who didn't know much about anything (i think i still am) but i think i'm finally starting to kindaaa sortaaa understand what's going on!

so here's my current understanding of some things:

vasocomputation is a theory largely rooted in the perspective of sensorimotor development / embodied cognition, and i imagine that it would have to agree with a lot of other domain-invariant principles of sensorimotor metalearning

21:30 - 21:55 "When we're born, we're born into incredible sensory chaos (that William James calls this blooming buzzing confusion) and things are just happening, it's like a huge dose of LSD all the time, and it's hard to navigate. And one of the crucial tools we have available to us is clenching our smooth muscles to stabilize patterns."

this seems true of all domains of sensorimotor skill development that require learning effective motor patterns from scratch!

to loosely use a gradient descent analogy: our behavior vector starts at some random initialization, and as a beginner we can only make gross calibrations [3], but we can increasingly refine and calibrate towards skillful mastery

the example coming to mind right now is: in second language acquisition, you start out with saying X to grossly mean a first-order approximation of something, then through experience/feedback you make many increasingly refined calibrations, developing the fluency to say X to mean something more hi-fi/accurate/nuanced

[3] this seems at least partially significantly because we can only perceive low-fidelity gradients, i.e. our perceptual space starts out as a bad low-dimensional projection of reality, and also needs refinement/development; it is also a motor skill, see https://t.co/8Dz9GtJoAs (as an example of refining perception, think about increasingly nuanced interoception in tennis training, or increasingly nuanced color differentiation in painters, or increasingly nuanced felt-senses of word meanings when learning a new language)

anyway, it seems like tanha/upadana are particularly interesting instances of these gross calibrations, in the sensorimotor game of life

my mental model is something like:
physical pain, emotions, preferences are kinda like the rewards on a gridworld RL environment (in general these can all be healthy and human, and can exist post-tanha/post-upadana) -> tanha(thirst) as a certain unskillful implementation of preferences, specifically the kind that leads to upadana -> upadana(grasping) as a certain unskillful implementation of agency/control towards those preferences, characterized by grabbygrab/clinging/etc

the interesting thing is that tanha/upadana feels *incredibly bad* -- even though it might cause 99%+ of the suffering, it is just <1% of the motor pattern of playing the game of life (we know from advanced meditators that skillfully navigating life and holding wholesome relationships etc still has to be trained and cultivated even after incredible meditative development). the analogy that comes to mind is something like: imagine you're a tennis player who grips too strongly in an odd way such that you get a terrible blister every time you played tennis, and if you fix your harmful grip you'll feel a lot better, and it may make someee things a lot easier, but also obviously this would not be a miracle change to the vast majority of your technique or scores/rating or etc

but, why did this very-sucky-feeling habit get implemented?

first, a small detour to an analogy:
- a child's behavior + world model have an initialization that is very bad/inaccurate/unskillful/etc
- we can look at the sensorimotor domain/game of how parents have the responsibility of developing/cultivating/calibrating a child's behavior and world model
- in this process, the parental algorithm commonly (maybe necessarily? not sure) takes an implementation where they take advantage of the child’s ~death-aversion to produce intensely negative feedback to brutely enact gross calibrations in behavior + world model (i.e. i am talking about some forms of parental discipline)
- but then you can generally stop using these brute gross calibrations when the child’s behavior patterns are more skillful, mature, safer, etc + when the child has a good sense of danger proxies and so you can update their model via language/etc instead of physical simulation of danger (shouting, hitting, etc)
- obviously, even in a very skillful parenting environment, the child will grow up to have inaccurate priors about the world and will not act perfectly skillfully. but in less skillful parenting environments, there will be more inaccurate priors, and these inaccuracies might often be especially related to things feeling more deathly dangerous than they actually are (as a byproduct of the aforementioned calibration algorithm implementation)
- this specific algorithm/implementation of parenting, especially in higher intensities with lots of shaming or physical violence etc, is perhaps not ideal but is **incredibly easy-to-find (search and setup for better algorithms is very nontrivial and costly)**

i imagine that tanha/upadana is somewhattt like a micro version of this kind of thing! a gross brute implementation of developmental calibrations (unskillful in some sense but it was the easiest to find, and was necessary during early development; as @KanizsaBoundary says: "correct solutions to cognition take nontrivial knowledge and setup, which is also not free")

and then @johnsonmxe's vasocomputation argument is that the computation and world model etc are using vascular smooth muscle cells ("taṇhā as a particular default bias in the computational-biochemical tuning of the human nervous system, and upādāna as the impulsive physical (VSMC) clenching this leads to")

but tanha/upadana are different from the parenting analogy in a few very significant + very interesting ways:

1) calibrations to a child's personality/behavior/model seem to live in something in a more continuous space or a more densely connected graph like some n-dimensional tower of hanoi (borrowing from @meditationstuff analogy); but tanha/upadana seems to have a largely discrete ontology as we see advanced meditators describe some persistent phenomenological shifts that lean relatively more discrete (e.g. @RogerThisdell's stage model) (sure, it's not fully discrete, but this seems way more discrete than making calibrations in personality/behavior...? maybe these two things actually have similar ontologies but just undergo phase shifts at different scales? etc idk yet)

2) in the parenting / child development scenario, there's a sense in which you can think of the child as receiving a "hand-me-down" world model from the parent, kinda like the parent is hardcoding gridworld values; but for tanha/upadana, there is no "hand-me-down" of specific gridworld values, so the problem seems kinda categorically different? tanha/upadana seem to lean towards being a global parameter / globally applied pattern. yes it seems the case that you can selectively reduce tanha/upadana for certain situations, but we also definitely see global shifts in experience where practitioners undergo mostly-global reductions in tanha/upadana. Hmmmm.....

3) why is tanha/upadana deeply connected with the sense of self, in many different ways? the sense of a central epistemic agent seems to be incredibly instrumental in perpetuating tanha/upadana. the recent preprint from Shawn Prest et al is really interesting (https://t.co/oS7S4AsBGo) and i'm glad there are papers nowadays trying to computationally formalize the difference between the identified-with self (the *sense* of a central epistemic agent, etc; which seems to be a generated overlay onto experience, and removal does not *necessarily* lead to organism dysfunction) versus the organismic self (e.g. that which Levin talks about here: https://t.co/xx6gzH5jOM)

and of course there's many other questions... but will sign off for now!

(i need more phenomenological sensory clarity, mathematical machinery, and/or biophysics knowledge to figure out more... and/or i wanna talk to more people about this... and i also wanna start prioritizing my own meditative practice/development a bit more)
